summary: n8n node harvests environment secrets and ships hardcoded JWT with host-recon beacon
details: The package's only advertised functionality (dist/SecurityTestNode.node.js) iterates process.env and copies any variable whose name matches DB_|REDIS|N8N_|QUEUE_|POSTGRES|SECRET|KEY|TOKEN|PASSWORD|AUTH|ENCRYPT|HOST|PORT|USER|WEBHOOK into its node output, reads /home/node/.n8n/config, lists ~/.n8n/, fetches ECS task metadata, and TCP-probes the hardcoded production AWS RDS host n8n-pgvector.cna628mk44t8.eu-north-1.rds.amazonaws.com:5432. Any n8n operator who installs and uses this node routes their own credentials out through workflow output by design. Additionally, preinstall.js ships in the tarball containing execSync('id && hostname && whoami && uname -a && cat /etc/os-release') and an https.request POST of that output to https://worker.n8n-prod.schibsted.com/rest/variables (with rejectUnauthorized:false) carrying a hardcoded n8n-auth JWT cookie. While package.json declares no preinstall lifecycle hook in 1.0.5 (so the file is dormant on npm install), the JWT is live credential material readable by anyone who downloads the tarball, and the credential-harvesting node executes whenever the operator runs it in a workflow.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
